encoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual encoder.h(3)

NAME

encoder.h - HEVC/H.265 encoder for ASCII art frames using libx265.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME 0x01
#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE 0x02

Functions


h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)
void h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t *ascii_data, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint32_t *avg_bitrate)

Detailed Description

HEVC/H.265 encoder for ASCII art frames using libx265.

Encodes terminal ASCII grid data using x265 for efficient compression. Handles variable-sized frames by storing dimensions in the encoded packet.

FRAME SIZE CHANGES: When terminal resize occurs (width/height change), the encoder must be reconfigured. This is handled transparently - the encoder detects size changes and reinitializes as needed.

PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][encoded_data: x265]

  • flags: Encoding flags (keyframe, etc.)
  • width: Frame width in characters
  • height: Frame height in characters
  • encoded_data: x265-encoded frame data

Definition in file encoder.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME 0x01

Encoder flags

Definition at line 33 of file encoder.h.

Referenced by h265_encode(), and h265_encoder_flush().

#define H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE 0x02

Definition at line 34 of file encoder.h.

Referenced by h265_encode().

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t * ascii_data, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Encode an ASCII art frame

The encoder automatically detects size changes and reinitializes when needed. The output packet includes frame dimensions so decoder can reconfigure.

Parameters

encoder Encoder handle
width Current frame width (characters)
height Current frame height (characters)
ascii_data Raw ASCII character grid (width × height bytes)
output_buf Buffer for encoded output
output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size

Returns

Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)

OUTPUT PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][x265_data...]

Definition at line 270 of file encoder.c.

271                                                                         {
272   log_dev("[H265_ENCODE_1] Starting h265_encode %ux%u", width, height);
273   if (!encoder || !ascii_data || !output_buf || !output_size) {
274     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder arguments");
275   }
276 
277   if (*output_size < 5) {
278     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
279   }
280 
281   log_dev("[H265_ENCODE_2] Calling h265_encoder_reconfigure");
282   asciichat_error_t result = h265_encoder_reconfigure(encoder, width, height);
283   log_dev("[H265_ENCODE_2b] h265_encoder_reconfigure returned: %d", result);
284   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
285     return result;
286   }
287 
288   h265_encoder_ascii_to_yuv420(ascii_data, width, height, encoder->yuv_buf);
289 
290   // Setup frame data pointers
291   encoder->frame->data[0] = encoder->yuv_buf;
292   encoder->frame->data[1] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height;
293   encoder->frame->data[2] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height + (width / 2) * (height / 2);
294   encoder->frame->linesize[0] = width;
295   encoder->frame->linesize[1] = width / 2;
296   encoder->frame->linesize[2] = width / 2;
297   encoder->frame->width = width;
298   encoder->frame->height = height;
299   encoder->frame->format = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
300 
301   // Request keyframe if needed
302   if (encoder->request_keyframe) {
303     encoder->frame->pict_type = AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I;
304     encoder->request_keyframe = false;
305   }
306 
307   // Encode frame
308   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Sending frame to encoder: %ux%u format=%d", width, height, encoder->frame->format);
309   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->frame);
310   if (send_ret < 0) {
311     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_send_frame failed: %d", send_ret);
312     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to send frame to encoder");
313   }
314 
315   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Receiving encoded packet...");
316   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
317   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
318     // EAGAIN is normal - encoder needs more frames before outputting a packet
319     log_dev("H265_ENCODE: EAGAIN - encoder buffering frames (normal), waiting for next frame");
320     *output_size = 0; // No output this time
321     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
322   }
323   if (recv_ret < 0) {
324     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d (EAGAIN=%d)", recv_ret, AVERROR(EAGAIN));
325     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive encoded packet");
326   }
327   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Got packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
328 
329   uint8_t flags = 0;
330   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
331     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
332     encoder->keyframes++;
333   }
334 
335   if (width != encoder->current_width || height != encoder->current_height) {
336     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE;
337   }
338 
339   size_t header_size = 5;
340   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
341   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
342 
343   if (required_size > *output_size) {
344     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
345   }
346 
347   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
348   output_buf[0] = flags;
349   output_buf[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
350   output_buf[2] = width & 0xFF;
351   output_buf[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
352   output_buf[4] = height & 0xFF;
353 
354   // Write encoded data
355   if (packet_size > 0) {
356     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
357   }
358 
359   *output_size = required_size;
360   encoder->total_frames++;
361   log_dev("[H265_ENCODE_DONE] Encoding complete, output_size=%zu", required_size);
362 
363   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
364 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, h265_encoder::codec_ctx,
    h265_encoder::current_height, h265_encoder::current_width,
    EAGAIN, ERROR_INTERNAL, ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE,
    ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, h265_encoder::frame,
    H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME, H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE,
    h265_encoder::keyframes, log_debug, log_dev,
    log_error, h265_encoder::packet,
    h265_encoder::request_keyframe, SET_ERRNO,
    h265_encoder::total_frames, and h265_encoder::yuv_buf.


Referenced by acip_send_image_frame_h265(),
    h265_client_capture_and_encode(), and
    threaded_send_image_frame_h265().






h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t
  initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)


Create a new x265 encoder for ASCII frames




Parameters


initial_width Initial frame width (characters)


initial_height Initial frame height (characters)


Returns


Encoder handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 39 of file encoder.c.


39                                                                                      {
40   if (initial_width == 0 || initial_height == 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Frame dimensions must be non-zero");
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   h265_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_encoder_t), h265_encoder_t *);
46   if (!enc) {
47     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate encoder structure");
48     return NULL;
49   }
50 
51   // Find best available HEVC encoder (try by ID first, then by name for specific implementations)
52   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
53 
54   // If generic HEVC encoder not found, try specific implementations by name
55   if (!codec) {
56     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("libx265");
57   }
58   if (!codec) {
59     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_vaapi");
60   }
61   if (!codec) {
62     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_videotoolbox");
63   }
64   if (!codec) {
65     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_nvenc");
66   }
67   if (!codec) {
68     codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name("hevc_qsv");
69   }
70 
71   if (!codec) {
72     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "HEVC encoder not found");
73     SAFE_FREE(enc);
74     return NULL;
75   }
76 
77   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
78   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
79     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate codec context");
80     SAFE_FREE(enc);
81     return NULL;
82   }
83 
84   // Configure codec context
85   enc->codec_ctx->width = initial_width;
86   enc->codec_ctx->height = initial_height;
87   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
88   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, 30};
89   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){30, 1};
90 
91   // Set ultrafast preset for low-latency encoding
92   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "preset", "ultrafast", 0) < 0) {
93     log_warn("Failed to set preset to ultrafast, using default");
94   }
95   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "tune", "zerolatency", 0) < 0) {
96     log_warn("Failed to set tune to zerolatency, using default");
97   }
98 
99   // Open codec (capture x265 library output during initialization)
100   int codec_open_result = 0;
101   LOG_IO("hevc", {
102       codec_open_result = avcodec_open2(enc->codec_ctx, codec, NULL);
103   });
104 
105   if (codec_open_result < 0) {
106     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to open HEVC encoder");
107     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
108     SAFE_FREE(enc);
109     return NULL;
110   }
111 
112   // Allocate frame and packet
113   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
114   enc->packet = av_packet_alloc();
115 
116   if (!enc->frame || !enc->packet) {
117     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
118     if (enc->frame)
119       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
120     if (enc->packet)
121       av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
122     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
123     SAFE_FREE(enc);
124     return NULL;
125   }
126 
127   enc->current_width = initial_width;
128   enc->current_height = initial_height;
129 
130   // Allocate YUV420P buffer (Y plane + U/V planes)
131   size_t stride = initial_width;
132   enc->yuv_buf_size = stride * initial_height * 3 / 2;
133   enc->yuv_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(enc->yuv_buf_size, uint8_t *);
134   if (!enc->yuv_buf) {
135     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate YUV buffer");
136     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
137     av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
138     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
139     SAFE_FREE(enc);
140     return NULL;
141   }
142 
143   log_info("FFmpeg HEVC encoder created: %ux%u (codec: %s)", initial_width, initial_height, codec->name);
144 
145   // Register encoder for debugging
146   NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER(enc, "h265_encoder", NULL);
147 
148   return enc;
149 }

    h265_encoder::current_height, h265_encoder::current_width,
    ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_MEMORY, h265_encoder::frame,
    log_info, LOG_IO, log_warn,
    NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER, h265_encoder::packet,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SET_ERRNO,

void
  h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Destroy an x265 encoder




Parameters


encoder Encoder to destroy (may be NULL)


Definition at line 151 of file encoder.c.


151                                                    {
152   if (!encoder)
153     return;
154 
155   if (encoder->frame) {
156     av_frame_free(&encoder->frame);
157   }
158   if (encoder->packet) {
159     av_packet_free(&encoder->packet);
160   }
161   if (encoder->codec_ctx) {
162     // Capture x265 encoder cleanup output (final frame stats, etc.)
163     LOG_IO("hevc", {
164       avcodec_free_context(&encoder->codec_ctx);
165     });
166   }
167   if (encoder->yuv_buf) {
168     SAFE_FREE(encoder->yuv_buf);
169   }
170   SAFE_FREE(encoder);
171 }






References h265_encoder::codec_ctx,
    h265_encoder::frame, LOG_IO, h265_encoder::packet,
    SAFE_FREE, and h265_encoder::yuv_buf.


Referenced by h265_client_context_create(), and
    h265_client_context_destroy().






asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush
  (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t *
  output_size)


Flush remaining buffered frames from encoder




Sends a NULL frame to the encoder to flush all buffered frames.
    This should be called before destroying the encoder or when you need to
    ensure all encoded data is retrieved.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


output_buf Buffer for encoded output


output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual encoded size


Returns


Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ASCIICHAT_OK with
  size=0 when no more data)


USAGE: // After final h265_encode() call, flush remaining
    frames while (size > 0) { size = max_size; h265_encoder_flush(encoder,
    buf, &size); if (size > 0) { // Send the flushed frame... } }


Definition at line 372 of file encoder.c.


372                                                                                                         {
373   if (!encoder || !output_buf || !output_size) {
374     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder or output parameters for flush");
375   }
376 
377   if (*output_size < 5) {
378     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
379   }
380 
381   // Send NULL frame to signal end of stream (flushes encoder)
382   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Sending NULL frame to encoder to flush buffered data");
383   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, NULL);
384   if (send_ret < 0) {
385     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_send_frame(NULL) failed: %d", send_ret);
386     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to flush encoder");
387   }
388 
389   // Try to receive a packet
390   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Attempting to receive flushed packet...");
391   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
392   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
393     log_dev("H265_FLUSH: EAGAIN - no more frames available");
394     *output_size = 0;
395     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
396   }
397   if (recv_ret == AVERROR_EOF) {
398     log_debug("H265_FLUSH: EOF - encoder is flushed");
399     *output_size = 0;
400     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
401   }
402   if (recv_ret < 0) {
403     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d", recv_ret);
404     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive flushed packet");
405   }
406 
407   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Got flushed packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
408 
409   // Check if packet size exceeds output buffer
410   size_t header_size = 5;
411   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
412   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
413 
414   if (required_size > *output_size) {
415     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
416   }
417 
418   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
419   uint8_t flags = 0;
420   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
421     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
422     encoder->keyframes++;
423   }
424 
425   output_buf[0] = flags;
426   output_buf[1] = (encoder->current_width >> 8) & 0xFF;
427   output_buf[2] = encoder->current_width & 0xFF;
428   output_buf[3] = (encoder->current_height >> 8) & 0xFF;
429   output_buf[4] = encoder->current_height & 0xFF;
430 
431   // Write encoded data
432   if (packet_size > 0) {
433     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
434   }
435 
436   *output_size = required_size;
437   encoder->total_frames++;
438 
439   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
440 }

Get encoder statistics




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


total_frames Output: total frames encoded


keyframes Output: total keyframes encoded


avg_bitrate Output: average bitrate in bits per second

void
  h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Request a keyframe on the next encode




Forces the encoder to produce an I-frame (keyframe) on the next
    call to h265_encoder_encode(). Useful for error recovery or stream
    synchronization.




Parameters


encoder Encoder handle


Definition at line 366 of file encoder.c.


366                                                             {
367   if (encoder) {
368     encoder->request_keyframe = true;
369   }
370 }
